title: rubus × adjunctus
intro: Rubus × adjunctus, also known as the hybrid raspberry or raspberry-blackberry hybrid, is a type of bramble that is a cross between a raspberry and a blackberry. The medicinal properties of Rubus × adjunctus lie in its anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of consuming Rubus × adjunctus include reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving cognitive function, and providing relief from symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Rubus × adjunctus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or jam, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
